heading: Training in democracy promotion and the protection of human rights
label: 8241 Training in democracy promotion and the protection of human rights
notes: Change of NameReferences to National Foreign Affairs Training Center considered to refer to George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center, see , set out as a note under .section 1(b) of Pub. L. 107–132section 4021 of this title

children.text: International documents and United States policy regarding the promotion of democracy and respect for human rights.

United States policy regarding the promotion and strengthening of democracy around the world, with particular emphasis on the transition to democracy in nondemocratic countries and democratic transition countries.

For any member, chief of mission, or deputy chief of mission who is to be assigned to a nondemocratic country or democratic transition country, ways to promote democracy in such country and to assist individuals, nongovernmental organizations, and movements in such country that support democratic principles, practices, and values.

The protection of internationally recognized human rights (including the protection of religious freedom) and standards related to such rights, provisions of United States law related to such rights, diplomatic tools to promote respect for such rights, and the protection of individuals who have fled their countries due to violations of such rights.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: b Consultation
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the National Foreign Affairs Training Center of the Foreign Service Institute of the Department, shall consult, as appropriate, with nongovernmental organizations involved in the protection and promotion of such rights and the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom with respect to the training required by this subsection.1So in original. Probably should be “section”.1

children.heading: Report
children.label: c Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report containing a description of the current and planned training provided to Foreign Service officers in human rights and democracy promotion, including such training provided to chiefs of mission serving or preparing to serve in nondemocratic countries or democratic transition countries.August 3, 2007
